title: ALO — Stack Health
description: Per-cluster operational health of the ALO pipeline: gateway, analyzer, and logstash.

panels.title: Worker Utilization
panels.description: Average Logstash worker utilization across the cluster. Near 100% = saturated.

panels.title: Max JVM Heap
panels.description: Worst-case Logstash JVM heap in the cluster.

panels.title: Backpressure
panels.description: Worst-case queue backpressure (0 = healthy, >0 = input blocked).

panels.title: Open FDs
panels.description: Worst-case file-descriptor usage. Approaching 100% = Logstash will crash.

panels.title: Analyzer Load
panels.description: Total in-flight analyzer requests across the cluster.

panels.title: Gateways Up
panels.description: Healthy gateway instances in the cluster.

panels.title: Pipeline Flow Rates
panels.description: Events/sec at each pipeline stage, summed across the cluster. If input >> output the pipeline is bottlenecked.

panels.title: Queue Depth
panels.description: Events waiting in Logstash queues across the cluster. Growing = input faster than processing capacity.

panels.title: Plugin Time per Event
panels.description: Avg seconds per event by plugin — shows whether the analyzer HTTP filter or the ES output is the bottleneck.

panels.title: Output Errors
panels.description: Elasticsearch bulk errors and non-retryable document failures. Sustained growth means data loss.

panels.title: Gateways UP
panels.description: Healthy vs total gateway instances over time.

panels.title: Request Rate
panels.description: Total gateway request rate across the cluster.

panels.title: Connection States
panels.description: Connection states summed across the cluster. High 'reading' = slow clients; high 'writing' = slow upstream.

panels.title: Dropped Connections
panels.description: Accepted minus handled across the cluster. Should be zero; non-zero means a worker_connections limit was hit.

panels.title: Events Dropped by Reason
panels.description: Events the gateway dropped (pipeline notification failure), broken down by reason.

panels.title: Event Delivery Rate
panels.description: Total events processed vs dropped per second. Healthy = dropped is zero.

panels.title: Request Rate
panels.description: Analyzer /analyze request rate across the cluster.

panels.title: Error Rate
panels.description: Fraction of non-2xx /analyze responses. The analyzer should always return 200 — any errors signal a real issue.

panels.title: Latency (P50 / P95 / P99)
panels.description: Request-duration percentiles aggregated across the cluster.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: Average analyzer process CPU across the cluster.

panels.title: Memory Usage
panels.description: Total analyzer resident memory across the cluster.

panels.title: Pipelines UP
panels.description: Healthy vs total Logstash pipelines over time.

panels.title: CPU Usage
panels.description: Average Logstash process CPU across the cluster.

panels.title: JVM Heap
panels.description: Logstash JVM heap used vs max, summed across the cluster.

panels.title: GC Pressure
panels.description: GC time rate. Sustained old-gen GC is the #1 cause of Logstash freezes.

panels.title: JVM Threads
panels.description: Logstash JVM thread count summed across the cluster.

panels.title: DLQ Document Count
panels.description: Documents in the dead-letter table for this cluster. Growth means the analyzer produced unindexable records.

targets.rawSql: SELECT toStartOfInterval(timestamp, INTERVAL $__interval_s second) AS time, count() AS "DLQ docs" FROM alo.alo_dead_letter WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND cluster_name = '${cluster}' GROUP BY time ORDER BY time

panels.title: DLQ Documents (Recent)
panels.description: Recent dead-letter documents for this cluster — what was rejected and why.

targets.rawSql: SELECT timestamp AS Time, error AS Error, request_path AS Path, request_method AS Method FROM alo.alo_dead_letter WHERE $__timeFilter(timestamp) AND cluster_name = '${cluster}' ORDER BY timestamp DESC LIMIT 20

panels.title: ES CPU
panels.description: Average Elasticsearch process CPU across the cluster.

panels.title: ES JVM Heap
panels.description: Elasticsearch JVM heap used vs max across the cluster.

panels.title: ES GC Rate
panels.description: Elasticsearch garbage-collection rate by collector.

panels.title: ES Thread Pool Queues
panels.description: Queued search/write operations. Growing queues = ES is the bottleneck.

panels.title: ES Rejected Operations
panels.description: Rejected thread-pool tasks per second. Non-zero = ES is overloaded and dropping work.

panels.title: Logstash Worker Utilization (per instance)
panels.description: Top 10 Logstash instances by worker utilization.

panels.title: Logstash JVM Heap (per instance)
panels.description: Top 10 Logstash instances by JVM heap usage.

panels.title: Analyzer In-Progress (per instance)
panels.description: Top 10 analyzer instances by in-flight requests.

panels.title: Gateway Request Rate (per instance)
panels.description: Top 10 gateway instances by request rate.

links.title: Stress Analysis
links.tooltip: Jump to ALO Stress Analysis dashboard
